title: 호랑이가 지었다고요?

goal.ko: 들은 이야기나 소문을 -다고 하다 / -(이)라고 하다로 전달할 수 있어요.
goal.en: I can retell stories and legends using indirect speech.

key_points: -다고 하다 — indirect speech for verbs/adjectives (I heard that... / they say...)
-(이)라고 하다 — indirect speech for nouns and names (it's called... / they call it...)
-아/어 버리다 — completed action, often with regret or finality (burned right down)
-아/어 놓다 — done in advance, left in that state (made and left there for us)

script.speaker: grandma
script.en: They say this temple was originally called Soraesa.

annotation.note:
V + 다고 해 = They say that ~ / I heard that ~
소래사였다고 해 = They say it used to be called Soraesa
= 다른 사람의 말을 전달
비가 온다고 해 = I heard it's going to rain

script.speaker: grandma
script.text: 그러다가 조선 후기부터 내소사라고 불러오고 있어.
script.en: And then, from the late Joseon period, it's been called Naesosa.

script.speaker: grandma
script.text: 임진왜란 때 대웅보전이 불에 타 버렸어.
script.en: During the Imjin War, the main hall burned right down.

annotation.note:
동사 + 아/어 버리다 = did it completely (gone!)
불에 타 버렸어 = burned down completely
= 완전히 끝나버린 것 (주로 아쉬움)
먹어 버렸어 = ate it all up

script.speaker: grandma
script.text: 그런데, 목공으로 변신한 호랑이가 다시 지었다는 이야기가 전해지고 있단다.
script.en: But the story passed down says that a tiger who transformed into a carpenter rebuilt it.

script.speaker: mirae
script.en: Oh come on, isn't that a story you made up, grandma?

script.speaker: grandpa
script.text: 그 호랑이를 대호선사라고 한단다. 큰 호랑이 스님이라는 뜻이야.
script.en: That tiger is called Daeho Seonsa. It means 'the great tiger monk'.

script.speaker: lia
script.text: 할아버지 이 절은 알록달록한 무늬가 없어요.
script.en: Grandpa, this temple doesn't have colorful patterns.

script.speaker: grandpa
script.text: 그걸 단청이라고 해. 호랑이가 바빴던 모양이구나.
script.en: That's called dancheong. The tiger must have been too busy.

script.speaker: lia
script.en: Even if he was busy, he should have finished the job!

script.speaker: mirae
script.text: 할머니 이거 보세요! 꽃이 너무 예뻐요!
script.en: Grandma, look at this! The flowers are so beautiful!

script.speaker: grandma
script.text: 그건 호랑이가 400년 후에 올 우리 보미를 위해 만들어 놓은 꽃다발이야.
script.en: That's the bouquet the tiger made and left for our Bomi — who would come 400 years later.

annotation.note:
동사 + 아/어 놓다 = did it and left it that way
만들어 놓은 꽃다발
= a bouquet made and left there (in advance)
↔ 버리다(완료·아쉬움) vs 놓다(준비·보관)

script.speaker: lia
script.text: 저기 처마 끝에 조그만 종이 달려 있어요.
script.en: There's a little bell hanging at the end of the eave over there.

script.speaker: grandpa
script.text: 저건 '풍경'이라고 하지. 댕댕 청아한 소리가 마음을 맑게 해 준단다.
script.en: That's called a punggyeong — a wind bell. Its clear, pure ring clears the mind.

script.speaker: mirae
script.text: 이 절은 왠지 마음이 편안해지는 느낌이에요.
script.en: For some reason, this temple gives me such a peaceful feeling.

items.english: Daeungjeon — the main hall of a Buddhist temple, where the Buddha statue is enshrined
dancheong — colorful decorative patterns painted on wooden temple buildings
punggyeong — temple wind bell · 댕댕 = the sound it makes
the eave — the overhanging edge of a traditional roof
to be passed down / to be handed down (stories, traditions)
to build / to write / to compose · 지어내다 = to make up / to fabricate
Imjin War (1592–1598) — Japanese invasion of Korea during the Joseon Dynasty
and then / after that / as time went on
to be colorful / multicolored and bright
to be clear and pure (of sound) · 청아한 소리 = a crisp, pure sound
to become peaceful / to feel at ease
for some reason / I'm not sure why but…

grammar.title: -다고 하다
grammar.title_en: Indirect speech (verbs/adj) — 'they say that…' / 'I heard that…'
grammar.explanation_en: Use -다고 하다 to report what someone said or what is widely believed — for verbs and adjectives. You're quoting a statement indirectly.
grammar.pattern: Action verb stem + -ㄴ/는다고 하다  ·  Adj stem + -다고 하다  ·  Past: -았/었다고 하다  ·  N + (이)라고 하다

scene_example.korean: 이 절은 원래 소래사였다고 해. / 호랑이가 다시 지었다는 이야기가 전해지고 있단다.
scene_example.english: They say this temple was originally called Soraesa. / The story passed down says a tiger rebuilt it.

rule_boxes.title: Present verb · 동사 현재
rule_boxes.content:
Action verb: stem + -ㄴ/는다고 하다
먹다 → 먹는다고 해 (they say he eats)
가다 → 간다고 해 (they say she's going)
Adj: stem + -다고 하다
맛있다 → 맛있다고 해 (they say it's delicious)

rule_boxes.title: Past tense · 과거형
rule_boxes.content:
Verb/Adj stem + -았/었다고 하다
먹었다고 해 (they say he ate)
맛있었다고 해 (they say it was delicious)
소래사였다고 해 (they say it was called Soraesa)

rule_boxes.title: Shortened forms · 줄임말
rule_boxes.content:
-다고 해 → -대 (casual)
맛있다고 해 → 맛있대!
-다고 하다 → -다 하다 (writing)
In conversation: 다고 해, 다고 하더라고요, 다고 들었어요

items.en: They say it will rain tomorrow.
I heard that restaurant is good.
They say this used to be the sea long ago.
They say a tiger built the temple.

grammar.title: -(이)라고 하다
grammar.title_en: Naming / quoting nouns — 'it's called…' / 'they call it…'
grammar.explanation_en: Use -(이)라고 하다 when reporting a name, title, or noun. This episode is full of it — naming the temple, the monk, the paintings, the bell.
grammar.pattern: N + 이라고 하다 [consonant ending]  ·  N + 라고 하다 [vowel ending]

scene_example.korean: 내소사라고 불러오고 있어. / 대호선사라고 한단다. / 단청이라고 해. / 풍경이라고 하지.
scene_example.english: It's been called Naesosa. / It's called Daeho Seonsa. / That's called dancheong. / That's called a punggyeong.

rule_boxes.title: Naming things · 이름 말하기
rule_boxes.content:
Vowel ending N + 라고 해:
내소사라고 해 (it's called Naesosa)
풍경이라고 하지 (it's called punggyeong)
Consonant ending N + 이라고 해:
대호선사이라고 → 대호선사라고 (contracts naturally)

rule_boxes.title: Asking for names · 이름 묻기
rule_boxes.content:
뭐라고 해요? / 뭐라고 하죠? (What's it called?)
이게 한국말로 뭐라고 해요? (What's this called in Korean?)
영어로는 뭐라고 해요? (How do you say it in English?)

rule_boxes.title: -다고 vs -(이)라고 · 핵심 비교
rule_boxes.content:
-다고 하다 = reporting a STATEMENT (verb/adj)
맛있다고 해. (They say it's delicious.)

-(이)라고 하다 = reporting a NAME or NOUN
비빔밥이라고 해. (It's called bibimbap.)

examples.en: What is this called in Korean?
That flower is called chrysanthemum.
This temple is called Naesosa.

grammar.title: -아/어 버리다  &  -아/어 놓다
grammar.title_en: Completed with feeling (버리다) vs Prepared in advance (놓다)
grammar.explanation_en: Both attach to verbs, but carry opposite feelings: 버리다 = done and gone (often regret or relief) / 놓다 = done in advance, left ready for later.
grammar.pattern: Verb stem (ㅏ/ㅗ) + -아 버리다 / -아 놓다  ·  Verb stem (other) + -어 버리다 / -어 놓다

scene_example.korean: 불에 타 버렸어. / 만들어 놓은 꽃다발이야.
scene_example.english: It burned right down (gone). / It's a bouquet he made and left there (for later).

rule_boxes.title: -아/어 버리다 · completeness + feeling
rule_boxes.content:
Action completed — with regret, relief, or finality:
먹어 버렸어. (I went and ate it all.)
잃어버렸어요. (I lost it — it's gone.)
다 써 버렸어. (I used it all up.)
임진왜란 때 불에 타 버렸어. (It burned right down during the war.)

rule_boxes.title: -아/어 놓다 · done in advance
rule_boxes.content:
Action done and left in that state (for later use):
만들어 놓은 꽃다발 (a bouquet he made and left there)
써 놓았어요. (I wrote it down already.)
준비해 놓았어요. (I've prepared it in advance.)
문을 열어 놓았어요. (I left the door open.)

rule_boxes.title: The key contrast · 핵심 차이
rule_boxes.content:
버리다 → done, over, with emotion (regret/relief)
놓다 → done and waiting, ready for later

도시락 먹어 버렸어. (I went and ate my lunchbox.)
도시락 싸 놓았어. (I packed my lunchbox and it's ready.)

examples.en: I went ahead and finished all my homework. (done — relief)
That person just left. (done — regret/finality)
I reserved it in advance (and it's all set).
It's a bouquet (that was) made in advance.

grammar.title: -더라도
grammar.explanation_en: Use -더라도 to say 'even if X were true, Y still holds.' It's stronger than -아/어도 (even if) in that it clearly concedes a hypothetical.

scene_example.english: Even if he was busy, he should have finished the job.

rule_boxes.title: Even if / Even though · 양보
rule_boxes.content:
바쁘더라도 연락은 해야지. (Even if you're busy, you should contact me.)
힘들더라도 포기하지 마세요. (Even if it's hard, don't give up.)
학생이더라도 존중받아야 해요. (Even as a student, you deserve respect.)

rule_boxes.title: -더라도 vs -아/어도 · 차이
rule_boxes.content:
-아/어도 = even if (neutral possibility)
비가 와도 갈 거예요. (Even if it rains, I'll go.)
-더라도 = even if (stronger counter-expectation, more formal)
비가 오더라도 반드시 갈 거예요. (Even if it rains, I will absolutely go.)

rule_boxes.title: Common patterns · 자주 쓰는 표현
rule_boxes.content:
힘들더라도 포기하지 마세요. (Even if it's hard, don't give up.)
바쁘더라도 밥은 먹어야 해요. (Even if you're busy, you need to eat.)
늦더라도 꼭 오세요. (Even if you're late, please come.)

examples.en: Even if it's hard, don't give up.
Even if you're late, please get in touch.

banmal_jondaemal.intro_en: How to pass on stories and legends — -다고 하다 / -(이)라고 하다 in context

rows.note: 리나's playful line — how an adult might say it politely

adult_dialogue.setup_en: Two adults share memories of visiting Naesosa.

lines.speaker: A
lines.en: What kind of temple is Naesosa?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: 아주 오래된 절이에요. 원래는 소래사였다고 해요.
lines.en: It's a very old temple. They say it was originally called Soraesa.

lines.speaker: A
lines.en: It's unusual that there's no dancheong.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: 그게 이 절의 특징이잖아요. 수수하지만 그래서 더 아름답다고 하더라고요.
lines.en: That's what makes this temple special, you know. They say it's plain, but that's exactly why it's more beautiful.

banmal_jondaemal.tip: When retelling stories or legends in Korean, -다고 하다 and -(이)라고 하다 are your go-to tools. Stack them naturally to sound very fluent!

items.tip:
Each syllable pronounced clearly:
임 / 진 / 왜 / 란
왜 = [왜] (not [와] — keep the ㅖ sound)

대 / 웅 / 보 / 전
Note: 웅 has ㅇ as 받침 — nasal [ng] sound, like 'Dae-UNG-bo-jeon'

편안 + 해 → 편아내…
안(ㄴ받침) + 해(ㅎ) → ㄴ connects forward: [펴난해] → [펴나내]
Same as: 편안하다 → [펴나나다]

quiz.question: What was the temple originally called?

quiz.audio_text: 그 호랑이를 대호선사라고 한단다. 큰 호랑이 스님이라는 뜻이야.
quiz.question: What is the tiger called, and what does it mean?
options: Daeho Seonsa — the great tiger monk
Daeho Seonsa — the white tiger spirit
Naeso Seonsa — the inner mountain monk

quiz.question: Which sentence uses -(이)라고 하다 correctly to give a name?
quiz.explanation: -(이)라고 하다 names a NOUN. 풍경 is a noun (the name of the bell). The other options use adjectives or verbs with -다고, which is incorrect for naming.

quiz.audio_text: 그건 호랑이가 400년 후에 올 우리 보미를 위해 만들어 놓은 꽃다발이야.
quiz.question: Who made the flower carving, and for whom?
options: The tiger made it for Bomi, who would come 400 years later
Grandma made it as a present for Bomi
Ancient craftsmen made it for the temple
The monk made it 400 years after the war

quiz.question: What is the difference between 타 버렸어 and 만들어 놓았어?
options: 타 버렸어 = burned and gone (with regret) · 만들어 놓았어 = made and left ready
타 버렸어 = burned slowly · 만들어 놓았어 = made quickly
Both mean something was done in advance

quiz.audio_text: 이 절은 왠지 마음이 편안해지는 느낌이에요.
quiz.question: How does 보미 feel at the temple?
options: A bit scared by the legend

real_life.title: 내소사는 어떤 절이에요?
real_life.passage: 내소사는 전라북도 부안군에 있는 천년 고찰이에요. 633년에 창건됐다고 해요. 가장 유명한 건 대웅보전의 꽃살문이에요. 나무로 만든 꽃무늬 문이 아주 섬세하고 아름다워요. 내소사는 단청을 하지 않아서 나무 본연의 색깔이 살아있어요. 입구에 있는 전나무 숲길도 유명해요.

messages.text: 응! 호랑이가 절을 지었다는 전설이 있다고 하더라.
messages.en: Yeah! Apparently there's a legend that says a tiger built the temple.
Really? What do they call the tiger?

messages.text: 대호선사라고 해. 큰 호랑이 스님이라는 뜻이래.
messages.en: They call it Daeho Seonsa. Apparently it means 'the great tiger monk'.

self_check: -다고 하다로 들은 이야기를 전달할 수 있어요 · I can report what I heard using -다고 하다
-(이)라고 하다로 이름을 말할 수 있어요 · I can name things using -(이)라고 하다
-아/어 버리다와 -아/어 놓다의 차이를 알아요 · I know the difference between 버리다 and 놓다
내소사의 특징 두 가지를 이야기할 수 있어요 · I can describe two features of Naesosa Temple
단청, 풍경, 꽃살문이 무엇인지 알아요 · I know what dancheong, punggyeong, and flower lattice doors are

usage.heading: -다고 하다 / -(이)라고 하다 — 간접 인용 (reported speech)
usage.content: Report what someone said without quoting them directly. The verb ending changes based on tense and type.

examples.en: She said it's going to rain tomorrow.
The teacher said the exam is difficult.
He said that person is Korean.

examples.ko: 저 절이 천 년 됐다고 해요.
examples.en: They say that temple is a thousand years old.

usage.heading: 간접 인용 공식 (Reported Speech Formula)
usage.content: Present action: -ㄴ/는다고 하다 · Adjective: -다고 하다 · Noun: -(이)라고 하다 · Past: -았/었다고 하다 · Command: -(으)라고 하다

examples.en: They say (someone) eats
They say it's cold
They say (she) is a student
They say (he) went

usage.heading: -아/어 버리다 vs -아/어 놓다
usage.content: -아/어 버리다: action is fully complete (often with relief or regret) · -아/어 놓다: action done in advance, result stays

examples.en: I went ahead and finished all my homework. (done, relief)
That person just went and left. (done, regret/surprise)
I reserved it in advance (and it's ready for us).
I made kimchi ahead of time (and it's stored).
